page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-144 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 144 dedicated vCPUs derived from 72 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 1116 GB of RAM, yielding 7.75 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. The processor features a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple domains. The instance achieves top 10% placement in multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, alongside top-tier memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached read/write operations. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as bzip3 decompression and Passmark encryption, also rank in the top 10%. For AI workloads, the hardware-assisted AMX instructions deliver top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large LLM models, though text generation speeds vary from average to strong depending on model size.

Given its high resource density, the c4-highmem-144 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and compute-heavy enterprise workloads. The lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is highly suited for large-scale database operations, memory-cached applications like Redis, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference or data science pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-144 is a memory-optimized server featuring 144 dedicated vCPUs and 1116 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.3 GHz with 72 physical cores, it includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the c4-highmem-144 delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and highly efficient database operations. It also demonstrates strong to top-tier capabilities in CPU-based LLM inference and cryptographic tasks. This server is optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale database management, Redis deployments, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-144 is a memory-optimized server with 144 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1116 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-144 features 144 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1116 GB RAM, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise and database workloads.
bullet_points: 144 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors
1116 GB of RAM providing 7.75 GB per core
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Best suited for large-scale databases, Redis, and CPU-based LLM inference
